RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden who was pulled up when a big-priced outsider for both hurdle runs last season.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who has shown some useful form on the Flat and was placed in a good-quality Newbury novice (2m, good to soft) on hurdle debut in March, having travelled strongly for a long way; had wind op in May; needs to be taken very seriously.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful on the Flat, albeit yet to add to French maiden win in spring 2024 and not at best for new stable this year; has good standard to reach on hurdling debut but could have a future over jumps; had wind op in April.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Formerly smart on the Flat (fourth in 2022 Derby) but was knocking about in low-grade handicaps last year; well beaten on stable/hurdle debut in May but shaped with much more promise when third over C&D (albeit well held in the end) later that month, and might collect another minor prize today.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten in a bumper and on both hurdling starts.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won on the Flat in France last year and had Listowel maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to yielding) at his mercy before last-flight fall on stable debut in May; first runner at this track for top Irish trainer Willie Mullins; obvious contender.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown some fair Flat form but became disappointing for David O'Meara last year and was soundly beaten in two 2m novice hurdles for new stable this spring.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won Irish maiden point in May but hasn't shown much on her two hurdling starts here (2m1f/2m6f) this summer, the latter on Saturday.

RaceName: Cumbria Grand Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed little in two points in spring and has struggled in two 2m6f novice hurdles here this summer, the latter on Saturday.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; dam French 7.5f AW winner, closely related to French 9.7f-12.5f winner Spiritueux, half-sister to French 1m/1m2f Group 1 winner Silasol, out of US 1m/8.5f Listed winner; top trainer won this race with a smart newcomer in 2021.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed some promise in two 6f novice runs on good ground in May; gelded since; his experience will be an asset but likely he will need improvement to take this.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Free-goer who won twice at Bangor last year (2m3f/2m7f); absent since consecutive heavy defeats last July/September, though, and returns with quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Useful on the Flat; back over hurdles for new stable this season, with by far the best of three runs a close second at Warwick (2m, good) two starts ago; pulled up over 2m1f since and not easy to predict over this new trip.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, including last two editions of this race; would appeal sightly more on ground slower than good but his fourth here (3m1f, good) two starts ago was a good effort and his well-held fifth over C&D last month came in a much stronger race than this; high on the list.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Won over 2m1f (good) here last summer and was second here on next two outings (2m6f/3m1f); not at same level this year (four runs) and was tailed off last time (2m1f) but can't be ruled out off current mark.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Unplaced all six hurdle starts; not disgraced when fourth of seven over about 2m6f (if additional yardage is included) at Market Rasen (good) last month, though, and may yet have a bigger run in him.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Came good at ninth attempt, drawing 13l clear to score over C&D (good) last month; hiked up 10lb for that and too inconsistent to rely upon heavily but still an obvious one to consider.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Placed in all three bumpers last year in 2024; did not show a great deal over hurdles last year but had soft ground as a plausible excuse when tailed off here (2m1f) on handicap debut a year ago; tries a new trip on this first start since; worth a glance in the betting.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Consistent in defeat last autumn and winter (2m3f-2m4f) but well below best since returning from three-month break this spring (three runs), and was pulled up over C&D on only previous visit here.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-9 in points and 0-7 under rules; tailed off on last month's chasing debut (1m7f), after a break, and now reverts to hurdling in new headgear; 9lb out of the weights; difficult to enthuse over.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing
Spotlight: Lots of ability for one racing at this level and he is on the same mark as when easily accounting for six rivals at Wolverhampton (6f) in March; not so good back on turf since but he can't be discounted; cheekpieces return having been worn once, unsuccessfully, in May 2022.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Three 6f AW wins for Iain Jardine in the spring; also ran well on turf at Hamilton in May but his form has dipped since; back to his last winning mark but he needs to raise his game; the removal of cheekpieces could be a positive.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden; promise on AW over the winter but he has suffered two heavy defeats in 6f handicaps (soft and good) this summer; drops in class but there are risks attached.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; quiet start for new stable but there was a welcome return to form when third of eight over C&D last week, keeping on and finishing in front of Water Of Leith; 3lb lower today and Danny Tudhope a good booking for the yard (three wins and three seconds from 14 rides); contender.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Some promise on first two starts (6f; 2yo debut for Ed Walker) but her two 7f runs, including handicap debut (after wind op) three weeks ago, have been less convincing; 4lb lower today but not a solid option.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Three 5f wins to his name, one of them at this track, but unplaced in six runs over 6f+; finished behind stablemate Whiskey Kisses over C&D last week; new cheekpieces not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; she made a promising seasonal reappearance when third of seven over C&D in May but that hasn't been a springboard to better things; Water Of Leith looks the yard's main hope.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Bumper winner; made quite promising start to hurdling career last autumn but looked out of sorts in two handicaps this spring (3m2f/2m7f) and it takes a leap of faith to support him here.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
OwnerName: Mrs G Jenkinson And Mrs B Butterworth
Spotlight: Regressive 7yo who had a disappointing campaign last season; returns from four-month break on a dangerous mark but was soundly beaten on both previous starts here and has quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner, most recently off today's mark at Huntingdon (3m1f, good to soft) in November; has become inconsistent, though, and dropped out quite tamely after making the running at Fontwell in May.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Seemed to have been aided by a wind op when placed at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) in April, his second handicap, but was pulled up on next two outings (latter was chase debut) and has since left Ben Pauling; has tongue-tie refitted for stable debut; cheekpieces removed after one use.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: In-form 7yo who comes here after two narrow wins at Market Rasen (both 3m+, good); latest 4lb rise ought to be manageable and this drop back in trip won't be a problem; big player if suited by this different track.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 2-17 over hurdles; not yet proven over quite this far but posted good effort when third at Perth (2m4f, good) this month and didn't run badly over 2m1f on only previous visit to Cartmel; could have a say.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Beaten over 19l when third over C&D last month and now 0-8 over hurdles but ran big race when second over C&D (good) on stable debut in May and has strong claims if judged on that performance.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-47 under all codes, having spent the bulk of his career in Ireland; flopped badly when favourite for recent stable debut at Stratford (2m2f, good to firm) but tries a new trip and sports first-time blinkers here; no surprise if Dan Skelton finds the key to him soon.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Returned from seven-month break with creditable third over C&D (good) in May and has obvious each-way claims if judged on that effort but Market Rasen last month was a backward step and he's now 0-14 over hurdles.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Belatedly ended a long losing run with a smooth defeat of Ramon Di Loria over C&D last week; looks well in under a 5lb penalty and his claims are obvious.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Hold-up performer who is thrown in on his 2025 form; yet to get going this time around but he wasn't beaten far at Carlisle 11 days ago and cheekpieces could give him a lift.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Prolific winner down the years, adding two AW handicaps to his haul in February; arrives in good form but he was no match for Imperial Guard here last week and the turnaround in the weights may be insufficient.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: D Jenkins J Glover R O'Donnell J Short
Spotlight: Won a 6f nursery at Redcar (good to soft) in September; slow start to this campaign but his third at Ripon (6f, good) was a personal best and the step up to 7f could spark further progress; high on the list.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: 14-race maiden; ran well when second of 11 at Catterick (7f, good) on his seasonal return in May but he failed to back it up at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) five weeks ago; others look stronger.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive in low-level 6f handicaps, winning at Newcastle in October and placed in both runs this summer; step back up to 7f can help; withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls here last month.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Resurgent 10yo who led close home at Perth (2m, good) last month and has now won three times since April; unlucky to come to grief when back at Perth last week and remains of strong interest, albeit he was well beaten over C&D on his only previous start here.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved to cause 25-1 surprise in C&D (good) novice last month, her fourth start over hurdles, but the value of that form is open to question (market principals disappointed) and her opening handicap mark probably demands further progress.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four hurdle wins, including this race in 2022; well held in C&D selling handicap in May, after a break, but last month's keeping-on second here was much better and he remains on a workable mark.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Took a while to get off the mark for current stable and had become expensive to follow but exploited much-reduced mark when winning stylishly at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good to firm) a week ago and still looks well treated under a 7lb penalty.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran well for a long way when third at Kelso (2m2f, good) in April but two lesser efforts have followed and he's now 0-7 for current stable; sometimes races too freely; others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Out of form for new stable this year and needs recent wind op to have made a big difference.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Now 9lb lower than when making all over C&D (good) last August but his form has slumped since and a revival is needed.

RaceName: Furness Building Society Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Swapped usual cheekpieces for refitted visor and gave good account when third from 3lb out of the weights over C&D (good) on Saturday; will effectively be 1lb lower if he turns out again but his 0-42 career strike-rate is not easily overlooked.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Made a winning stable debut at Pontefract (6f, good) in April; never featured at Epsom on Oaks day but he was back in form when third of ten at Thirsk 19 days ago; each-way shout.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
OwnerName: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover
Spotlight: Running well in 6f handicaps this summer, beating a big field at York in May and running to a similar level in defeat twice since; no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well again.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten in three handicaps for new stable this year, the latest over a mile nine days ago; latest 5lb drop in the weights is not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
OwnerName: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell
Spotlight: Not won since his stellar campaign in 2024 but there has been promise this year, including at Hamilton on Friday; 2lb lower today and now sports cheekpieces for the first time; chance.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well over C&D last September; beat 17 rivals when landing a Class 4 handicap at Ascot (6f, good to firm) in May but he was unable to build on it at Pontefract last month; perhaps vulnerable once more.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: The bulk of his winning has been done on AW but he did win a 5f handicap at the Curragh last August; recent efforts need bettering but he would seem likely to give his running.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Another who has a better strike-rate on AW than turf; not beaten far into fourth in a 5f handicap at Doncaster 17 days ago, a race where he fluffed the start; yet to shine at Ayr; one of three runners for Jim Goldie.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well over C&D on her penultimate start; 2-1 joint favourite for a 6f fillies' handicap at Carlisle 11 days ago, making some late headway into fourth; should run her race once again.

RaceName: Fergie & Myra Happel Ayr Gold Cup Trial Handicap
OwnerName: John Murphy & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: Useful on AW on his day but 0-21 on turf; handicapped accordingly but his latest C&D 12th didn't suggest he was the answer to today's puzzle.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Very useful in his prime and enjoying a revival this year, with the latter of two wins gained here (3m5f, good) in May; last month's C&D third was perfectly respectable and he ought to be right in the thick of things again.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Left clear at final fence to exploit reduced mark at Down Royal (2m4f, good to yielding) on Boxing Day 2024 and will be fine over this longer trip but this is his first run since and he's a 12yo now.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Former pointer/hunter chaser who won a Sedgefield handicap (3m2f, good to soft) off 2lb higher for Ruth Jefferson in January; not quite in same form on first four starts for new stable and fell on latest outing a week ago but it's difficult to put a line through him.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: In good form in the spring, winning at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) before near misses at Thirsk and over C&D; not at his best on his last two starts but this is an ease in class and he's a major player in his bid to win this race for a second time (successful in 2024).

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 on the up and she has maintained her progress in 2026, winning four times over 1m1f on good ground; not at her best last time but foolish to think she won't continue progressing; unraced on faster than good.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: Won four handicaps here (1m-1m2f, good or slower) in the second half of 2025; yet to fire this time around and may need both slower ground and further help from the handicapper.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a losing run when beating 11 rivals at Ripon (1m, good) last month; respectable efforts since without suggesting she was ahead of her mark.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: No turf win since 2022 but he has won three AW handicaps in the last 14 months; should be well treated but recent efforts haven't been his best; this is a drop in class so not dismissed, even though the return of cheekpieces wouldn't be an obvious plus based on previous usage.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: Gained first win since his 2yo days when making all in a Class 6 over C&D last week (blinkered first time); unlikely to get a soft lead this time and a 5lb penalty against stronger opposition demands more in any case.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: Two solid runs to begin his time with this yard and the addition of cheekpieces saw him win a 1m1f handicap at Musselburgh three weeks ago; only 2lb higher and he should give it another good go.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
OwnerName: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: Beat Donna Nook over C&D in May when rated 1lb higher; put a couple of lesser efforts behind him when chasing home Runninsonofagun here last week; 7lb better off for 2l defeat so has a handicapping chance; new visor replaces cheekpieces; others still more appealing.

RaceName: John Hamilton Scotrail Celebration Handicap
Spotlight: Won three in a row over 1m and 7f at Musselburgh (all on good) last June and July but she wasn't seen after August and two runs this year have been poor; risky.

RaceName: Weatherbys Design & Print Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won novice hurdle in April and is 3-3 over fences since, the latest win gained in three-runner contest at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last month; may still have more improvement to come if aided by first-time cheekpieces but contests a stronger race here and the drop back in trip is not sure to suit.

RaceName: Weatherbys Design & Print Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Signed off for Twiston-Davies stable with creditable second behind a next-time-out winner over hurdles in May, having made the running in first-time cheekpieces; last throughout on stable debut without headgear in four-runner Newton Abbot chase this month but the cheekpieces are back on here and it will be interesting to see whether connections revert to more positive tactics.

RaceName: Weatherbys Design & Print Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Slightly disappointing when weakening fifth on last month's chasing debut at Hexham but now 2lb lower than when close third over hurdles here in May and will be dangerous if able to transfer that form to fences.

RaceName: Weatherbys Design & Print Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Delivered on promise shown in Irish points/bumpers when making all twice over hurdles in novice company at Hexham (2m/2m4f, good) in May; respectable third on handicap debut there (2m4f, good) last month and looks quite interesting on this chase debut, even from 4lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: 11-race maiden; only one effort of note since joining Jim Goldie but he drops into his first 0-60 having undergone wind surgery since last seen; interesting to see how strong he is in the market.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Back to the mark off which he won at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm) last August; finished third in his first two runs this year but not at the same level on his last two starts; drops in trip/grade and could bounce back to form.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last month; not so good in either run since, the latest over C&D two weeks ago; not ruled out but not the most solid option either.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Not fired in two runs back from a 399-day absence, the latest here (1m) last week; has a C&D win on his record but others appeal more.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice over 7f when trained by George Scott; initially struggled in Ireland but finished second in a couple of handicaps at around 7f last month; beaten favourite over 7f here two week ago, after which he left Maurice Ahern; stamina for 1m2f has to be taken on trust and he goes without headgear on his stable debut.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who was recording his 12th win at the track when leading close home over 1m5f last week; able to race off a 3lb lower mark today and he has to be of interest from a handicapping perspective.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Third in 1m4f handicaps at Musselburgh on her last two starts, staying on nicely on both occasions; drop back in trip on faster ground isn't sure to suit.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
OwnerName: J D Wright & Mrs S Wright
Spotlight: Second of nine at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm) 18 days ago, after which he was sold out of Harry Charlton's stable for 18,000gns; should have more to offer over this trip; interesting.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran with plenty of course form to his name; third of nine over 1m here last week (winner Runninsonofagun runs in the 4.00); one of three runners for the stable.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up in two of her four handicaps; her form dipped at Nottingham 18 days ago but no surprise to see her bounce back with a big run.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well from out of the handicap at Catterick (1m4f, good to firm) last week, poaching a big lead but reeled in close home; whether she can repeat that form in this bigger field remains to be seen.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Led close home to cause 25-1 surprise over C&D (good) in May, form that has been very well advertised by the placed horses (one was Kingofthegame) since; not in same form over hurdles last month but still strongly considered; wears first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Bettered low-key stable debut (over hurdles) when second to very progressive rival over C&D (good) last month and remains well handicapped on some of his form for Jamie Snowden; likely contender.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
OwnerName: T J Stubbins and M E Sowersby
Spotlight: Placed three consecutive times in March-May (2m4f-2m5f, good/good to soft) and has claims if judged on that form but didn't fire when well-held third of four on latest outing and also disappointed on only previous visit to Cartmel; usually ridden patiently; others appeal more.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Dennis J Coppola & Mrs Dianne Sayer
Spotlight: Placed in two 2m7f Kelso chases in the autumn (good/good to soft) but ran poorly over 3m1f here on Saturday, after a five-month break, and will need to rebound quickly.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Gained all three chase wins at this track (2m5f-3m1f); bounced back from two lacklustre efforts when staying on to go close here (3m1f, good; usual tongue-tie/blinkers left off) on Saturday and remains well handicapped but it's hard to predict whether he'll be in the right mood again; wears cheekpieces for first time since 2024.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 1-16 over hurdles, having gained a hard-fought course win (2m6f, good) in May; evidently well suited by this track but will need to find hidden reserves to overcome a 4lb higher mark on this chasing debut.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Well handicapped on old form but failed to complete three consecutive races in April-June and was well beaten here (2m1f) on Saturday; difficult to enthuse over.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: One of two to pull clear of the others when opening account at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) in March, and both subsequent fourths (3m/3m1f; latter was here) were perfectly respectable; may benefit from this drop back in trip; no surprise if he features.

RaceName: Old Park Wood Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Well beaten on both starts this year (3m1f/2m3f) and makes no appeal from 10lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Steady progress in three novice/maiden runs and he took another step forward when second of nine on his handicap debut at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago (finished just in front of Soul Warrior who runs in the 3.00); open to further improvement and looks quick enough for 5f.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: 5f AW win in December; knocking at the door this year, including when a close second on his return to turf at Thirsk (5f, good) last month; now 2lb higher but likely to give his running.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Made all to win a 5.5f handicap at Wetherby last month; unable to build on it at Windsor 11 days later but she still has low mileage.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Having dropped in the weights he ran his best race of the year when third of nine at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) on Thursday; now has something to build on.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Improved for the addition of a visor when easily seeing off six rivals in a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton in March; well beaten on next three starts and sold out of Robert Cowell's stable for 2,200gns in May; fine effort when short-headed on his stable debut at Musselburgh three weeks ago and a repeat would see him go well despite a 2lb rise.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Redcar (5f, good to firm) in May off a 2lb lower mark and she followed it with a good third at Ripon; played up beforehand and then in the stalls when disappointing at Beverley four weeks ago; needs to show that temperamental display was a one off.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: Won twice in Britain with cut in the ground; after a spell over hurdles, didn't show much on return to the Flat at Killarney; a bit to prove for now and might prefer softer conditions.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: Close third at Limerick in June 2025 beginning to look like a flash in the pan; poor form over hurdles and on the Flat this year; tailed off at Fairyhouse latest (1m6f, good).

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: C&D winner for Joseph O'Brien; no impact over hurdles for this yard; modest effort in 1m4f handicap at Tramore on return from 98 days off latest; cheekpieces go on and drops into claiming company for yard with good record in these races.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: In-form filly arrives in search of a hat-trick after knuckling down to win claimers at Bellewstown (1m, good) and Killarney (1m, good); this longer trip is a query, but should be on the premises again if she stays (stamina in pedigree but ran poorly over 10.5f before).

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: On a long losing run and no sign of it ending in recent starts; tailed off at Bellewstown latest and rated just 34 now; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: 0-12 on turf on the Flat; ran respectably in a couple of handicaps earlier this season, but last three runs haven't been great; could do better now dropped into claiming company, but needs to leave behind recent form.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: Maiden has shown promise in a couple of his runs; not beaten that far when a 5.25l third in 1m4f Tramore claimer latest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); rated 69, so best treated by race conditions and this drop in trip should suit; can't discount.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: Minor promise in a couple of 7f/1m handicaps last October on soft/heavy off basement mark; toiling this season, however, and impossible to fancy on recent showings.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee Never Beaten By SP Claiming Race
Spotlight: Well backed both starts for this stable; kept on well over 7f at Limerick for a never-dangerous sixth; had an excuse (coughing) when soundly beaten at Leopardstown latest (1m1f, god); tongue-tie (worn on best runs for former yard) returns and also sports blinkers; interesting.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Well beaten in maidens over further at Fairyhouse and Tramore; needs this for a mark.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Two decent efforts in ordinary maidens over 1m2f and 1m4f at Dundalk; steps back up in trip after an ordinary effort in an ordinary Killarney maiden last week; should fare better here.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Second to a subsequent winner in 1m4f optional claimer at Limerick last month; predictably outclassed by 92-rated foe, who was 1-5, at Killarney last week, but handicapper gave her a 3lb rise all the same; step up in trip will suit.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Staying on third on his handicap debut over 1m at Cork last month; step up to this trip looks suitable, but might just find one or two too good.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Poor performer on all known form; step up in trip unlikely to lead to an upturn in fortunes.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Fared okay after a slow start on debut at Fairyouse over 1m2f; back up to a similar trip here after failing to build on that over 1m at the Curragh.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: No show in three runs in maidens over 7f to 1m; steps up in trip here and hard to ignore completely coming from these quarters; needs a lot more though.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Down the field in a pair of better maidens; hard to make a case for here.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Not entirely disgraced on debut in a Cork maiden on debut in May; should be more effective once getting a handicap mark.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Some encouraging word for her before her debut at Dundalk in November and ran with a deal of credit behind a Listed-class winner; reportedly failed to handle the softer ground at Cork in May; tongue-tie tried here and probably still some upside to this filly; has an ideal opportunity here.

RaceName: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Maiden
Spotlight: Not a bad level of form in two maiden outings; might have finished closer but for shipping a bump in a 1m3f maiden at Killarney last week; looks the stable second string here, though.

RaceName: A1R Services Ltd Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Promising third on Newmarket debut (6f, good) in May; ran respectably when in midfield in Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot next time and she had another stiff task in a Group 2 at Newmarket ten days ago; this is more realistic and she needs watching in the market on nursery debut.

RaceName: A1R Services Ltd Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form in his first two starts but he found plenty of improvement when a close fourth of ten at Doncaster (6f, good to firm; 20-1) 17 days ago; needs to build on that but it's still early days and he's a half-brother to a useful sprinter; in the mix on nursery debut.

RaceName: A1R Services Ltd Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: The Lamb Inn Wantage Racing Team
Spotlight: Comfortable winner at Bath (5.6f, firm) in May but that was a small-field maiden and he struggled in the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) last time; now back in calmer waters on quicker ground but he has a mixed record so far and needs to kick on again after a gelding operation.

RaceName: A1R Services Ltd Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise in her first two runs before battling well to beat the two market leaders in tight finish at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last month; should have more to offer now switched to a nursery and she's a key player.

RaceName: A1R Services Ltd Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Faded into fifth in his first two runs but he kept on well for a last-gasp success in a Bath novice (5.6f, firm) 19 days ago; the runner-up's rider had some issues with the reins in the closing stages but this well-bred colt is going the right way and he's respected on nursery debut.

RaceName: A1R Services Ltd Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Weakened into a remote fourth on Haydock debut (6f, soft) in May but he left that form behind when runner-up to an emphatic winner at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; that form has been boosted by the third since and he needs a close look on nursery debut.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Three nondescript runs in maidens over 1m4f; drops back in trip for her handicap debut and would not appear flattered by her opening mark; interesting nonetheless.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: No show in three maidens and few chances have been taken with his opening mark; looks up against it.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Far from disgraced in a maiden and a handicap on soft ground in the spring; step up to 1m4f proved beyond her in a Leopardstown handicap in May; champion jockey booked and clearly better is expected here, back down in trip.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Showed a little ability in three runs in maidens; beaten out of sight in a Fairyhouse claimer since; others preferred.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up in a heavy ground nursery at Galway last October; failed to cut much ice on her seasonal bow at Down Royal last month; slower ground and probably a longer trip might be more of an optimum for her; best watched.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Not a huge amount of encouragement from three runs in maidens; likely to do better in this sort of race, but a watching brief looks the prudent option here.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Best run when fourth in a fast-ground handicap at Leopardstown last month over a similar trip; needs to bounce back here, but has the plum draw and certainly respected on that form.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: No show in three maidens and never competitive on her handicap debut over 1m1f at Leopardstown, despite not being beaten far; cheekpieces tried this time.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Overcame plenty of adversity when a strong-finishing third over C&D last month; experience of this track could be invaluable and has a very good draw, if able to get away on terms this time.

RaceName: Download The Tote App Handicap
Spotlight: Modest level of ability at a very low level; 5lb wrong here and unlikely to figure.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Didn't live up to market expectations on Yarmouth debut last month but she left that form a long way behind when beating a clear second at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago; has a penalty but she showed a good attitude last time and this well-bred filly is open to more progress; key player.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 8,500gns foal, £55,000 yearling; third foal; Harry Angel half-sister to 8.3f winner Sylkie (RPR 77); dam 1m2f winner (79), closely related to 1m4f winner Caledonian Crusade, out of 1m2f winning half-sister to 10.5f Group 3 winner Dance Dress; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling by Showcasing; 12-1 on Newbury debut (6f, good to firm) last month and she ran green before finishing a well-held seventh of ten; should know more this time but she needs a major step forward.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; second foal; half-sister to 6f winner Flash Harry (RPR 106); dam sprint maiden (51), half-sister to 5f/6f winners Benny And The Jets and Wiley Post, out of 6f winning sister to Listed-placed 6f winner Poppy Seed; yard 23% with 2yos this season and she's an interesting newcomer; tongue-tie on.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Out of Irish 1,000 Guineas/Yorkshire Oaks winner Pleascach; well-held sixth in both runs, but it was a step forward at Epsom (7f, good to firm) 11 days ago and she shaped as though this drop back in trip could suit; not ruled out.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 28,000gns breeze-up 2yo; closely related to Italian 6f-7.5f winner Dottor Misagu (including 2yo); dam Italian 7.5f 2yo Listed winner, half-sister to winners Raneenn (6f including 2yo) and King Of Scotia (7f/1m including 2yo); plenty to like on paper but yard is 1-20 with 2yos this season.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Didn't show much on her Sandown debut but she kept on well when fourth of ten at Newbury (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; that was a promising effort and she could be dangerous if she can build on that.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-sister to three winners including Whenthedealinsdone (5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 102); wasn't beaten far when sixth on Kempton debut in May but she found plenty of progress when a clear third at Newbury (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; didn't get much luck in that race and could be a big factor if she gets the breaks this time.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling, 70,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; Havana Grey half-brother to 4.5f-6f winner Fondest Dream (including 2yo/US); dam maiden (RPR 56), sister to 6f Group 3 winner Keystroke; judged on sales information and the stable he represents, looks the pick of the newcomers.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 37,000gns yearling, 30,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo by Sioux Nation and first foal of unraced half-sister to Group-placed 7f 2yo winner Sparkling Beauty; yard is 0-34 with 2yos in last five seasons.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showed some promise over 6f on debut, without quite getting home; fared better over C&D (good to firm; finished second of seven) since and is evidently going to take after her 5f-winning dam; one of the main form players.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Has shown signs of ability at Bath (5.7f) and Chester (6f) while looking the type who'll be more interesting in nurseries shortly; chance today partly depends on whether the drop to a bare 5f prompts improvement.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Brings the worst form having finished last of five, achieving little, in 5f contest at Nottingham; looked green but he needs to improve markedly for that run.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Absent and gelded since coming home last of the seven finishers at Thirsk in early May; that race has worked out well and he's half-brother to a multiple 5f winner for his yard, so improvement is plausible.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Effectively the most exposed contender, having raced five times; placed on three occasions, including twice over C&D; creditable second at Hamilton most recently; reasonably solid but figures in the 60s reflect that he's ordinary.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: 10,000euros yearling; by Invincible Army; sibling to winners in Italy (5f/6f, including 2yo) and Poland (1m/1m2f); dam unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Dark Reckoning; check the betting.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Changed ownership for £380,000 prior to finishing 16th of 27 in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot, running respectably on the figures despite ending up further behind Pershaada than had been the case at Goodwood (5f, good) where she was an eye-catching second amid traffic issues; the form pick.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 37,000euros yearling; by Ten Sovereigns and first foal of unraced half-sister to useful 6f 2yo winner Zumbi and daughter of unraced sister to smart sprinter Land Of Dreams; heed the market signals.

RaceName: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Invincible Army filly; dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Mannaal (1m-11.4f) and Bilhayl (5f), out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; probably best watched in this field on debut.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
OwnerName: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick
Spotlight: Seven career wins and probably not as dependent on soft ground as he once was; consistent this season without really threatening to win and likely to run his race again.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Started the season with victory in a 1m handicap at Cork and has kept his form well since without winning; close second over that trip at Bellewstown last time saw him go up another 2lb; new headgear combination tried here and unlikely to be far away; draw is the main negative

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: AW winner for Charlie Johnston last year,  but hardly a glimmer of form for her current connections; makes no appeal at present.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: In good form this season without winning; just touched off over this trip at Gowran and a close second over 1m3f at Naas; needs to bounce back after a below-par effort at Killarney last week but every chance that he will; cheekpieces tried.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Fair third off 70 in a heavy-ground maiden over this trip at Gowran last October; handicap mark going the right way, but overall form disappointing; the drop back in trip could be a positive.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Good level of form last summer culminated in a deserved success in a 1m2f handicap at Navan; very regressive this season, though, and hard to fancy on current evidence.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first win for almost three years in a 1m Gowran handicap in April; failed to cut much ice in two efforts since; might just prefer it softer these days.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent enough filly; touched off in a heavy ground maiden over 1m2f for John O'Donoghue; seemed to see out longer trips quite well on her last two runs, here and at Tramore; drop back in trip  not certain to suit, but certainly respected.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Decent operator at a level on turf and AW, his most recent win coming in a 1m2f Dundalk handicap in January last year; not seen since third in a Tipperary handicap 14 months ago; market may prove the best guide.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Quite lightly raced since winning this race off 68 two years ago; encouraging effort after more than nine months off in a 1m4f handicap at Tramore two weeks ago; foolish to discount.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Six career wins and undergoing a real new lease of life this season with victories in handicaps at Killarney and Leopardstown; 11lb rise for those two wins is not excessive and every chance of going close once more.

RaceName: Daily Sports Special At Tote.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Plenty of ability but has two ways of running; at her best when winning a competitive 1m heat at Gowran in June, much less so off a 7lb higher mark at Leopardstown 17 days later

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Struggled in two bumpers and he's been beaten 16l at big prices in both Flat runs (1m2f) this summer.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 375,000gns foal; gelded brother to 1m2f/1m4f winner Bay Bridge (including Group 1; RPR 125), half-brother to winners Stormy Sea (1m; 90) and Nataleena (1m5f-2m; 73); dam sprint maiden (59), half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Shimraan; lots to like on paper and he needs watching in market.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 125-1 and he finished a tailed-off last on recent Chepstow debut (7f, good to firm).

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: RC Syndicate X and Gary Gillies
Spotlight: 90,000gns half-brother to a 1m3f winner in Germany; well held in sole 2yo run but he's been knocking on the door in novice events at York (1m, good) and Thirsk (1m, good to firm) this season; sets a clear standard on that form and he's open to more progress at this new trip; strong contender.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 330,000gns foal, 500,000gns yearling; second foal; half-brother to 7f/1m AW winner Breakdancer (RPR 87); dam 5.7f/6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 106), half-sister to useful 1m4f/1m5f Flat and 2m hurdle winner Nessun Dorma; interesting newcomer and he needs a close look.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 150-1 and beaten out of sight in a novice at Salisbury (7f, good) last October; gelded since and can only be watched on return for new yard.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 210,000gns yearling; third foal; gelded half-brother to winners Lava Stream (1m/1m2f including Listed; RPR 107) and Iron Lion (1m2f/1m4f; 103); dam 1m 2yo/1m2f winner (95), half-sister to winners Journey (1m4f Group 1) and Mimikyu (14.5f Group 2); one of two newcomers for Godolphin and interesting to see how he figures in the market.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 100,000euros yearling; second foal; dam twice-raced (runner-up at 1m4f; RPR 76) half-sister to winners Pakistan Star (Hong Kong 1m2f/1m4f Group 1) and Ninas Terz (useful German 6f 2yo); has good standard to aim at on debut but she needs watching in market.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Tailed off at big prices in both runs (1m6f/1m3f) this summer, with RPRs in the 30s on both occasions.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Has gained both wins over 6f, including narrowly at Carlisle (good to firm) last time; back up only 3lb he may remain competitive, provided this drop to 5f proves no issue; opening mark was 13lb higher.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
OwnerName: J R Dwyer And B Smart
Spotlight: Has made the frame plenty of times since AW maiden win, close third at Thirsk most recently; 0-16 in handicaps but holds a fighting chance off unaltered mark, which is 12lb lower than opening one.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner down the years but is 0-7 this term and her form has declined to some degree, as current mark reflects; drops back to Class 6 for the first time in a while.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner in previous seasons; 0-11 this year but is proving consistent, close second returned to AW most recently; remains 1lb below last winning mark; in the mix.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Struggled in Class 4 event last time; in good form in Class 5 otherwise of late, including C&D success in June; drops back to Class 6 but still needs to prove he can win off marks higher than the 50s.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed in small field at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) last time, taking record in blinkers to 3-9; back up 4lb but Jack Callan takes off useful 3lb; has won over C&D; enters calculations.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Retains ability aged eight; made all at Nottingham (5f, soft) last time, building on his reappearance effort and registering an eighth success; has never gained back-to-back wins, though.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Bounced back to form at Leicester (5f, good to firm) but beat only two rivals and has gone back up 5lb for that win; returns to a deeper field, albeit has landed the plum draw.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Far from consistent this season but got up in the final strides over C&D (good to firm; off 1lb higher) in May; possibilities provided he's back in similar form and the race again pans out favourably.

RaceName: Happy Birthday Katie Exon Handicap
Spotlight: Scored on AW in April; inconsistent otherwise of late; has plenty of C&D form, including in this contest, but posted a substandard effort returned to this scenario last time.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: A winner of ten races over hurdles and on the Flat; scored in the Cork Derby (1m4f, soft) on most recent start in this sphere; just 4lb higher now, handles better ground and has run well over hurdles here before; okay run over fences latest and can't discount back on the level.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Hold-up sort signed off last season with a 12.5f win at Thurles for Dermot Weld; winning start for this yard at Leopardstown in May over 1m2f; has gone up 14lb for those two wins, but could continue to progress; chance.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: No win since 2023; big chance off this mark on best runs over 1m6f/1m4f last season and though well beaten in both runs this term, they were over an inadequate trip; this distance much more suitable and Colin Keane takes over; interesting.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time AW winner in France is just 1-10 on turf; has failed to beat a rival in first two runs for this stable; needs to revive upped markedly in trip.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose mare arrives in top form after back-to-back wins over C&D (yielding) and Tramore (1m4f, good) in lower grades; has gone up a total of 13lb but the form of her latest win has since been franked by the runner-up so there could yet be more to come; top rider booked.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Promise in maidens and far from disgraced on handicap debut at Navan (1m2f, good) where a 3l fourth of 11 in first-time blinkers (retained); could go well if he stays this longer trip, but pedigree raises questions.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden winner; below par first two runs this term (including when well held behind Londonofficallin in Cork Derby); step in the right direction when 3l third behind Daboya at Tramore latest in first-time tongue tie (retained); mark unchanged and place claims if backing that up.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Dangerous when gets his favoured soft conditions, as he did when bolting up at Bellewstown in October; however, he's never won off a mark this high, he lacks a run and this ground may be on the lively side.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise in a couple of starts for former yard in France; bought for E40,000 at Arqana last July and is likely best watched on stable debut after a 407-day absence, unless the market speaks.

RaceName: Tote.ie Everyday Is A Money Back Day Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time winner, five of those coming on turf; ground-versatile and showed signs of returning to form when coming from the back for a 6.75l fourth of 15 at Leopardstown on latest; eased 2lb but has not run well on both previous visits to this venue.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Four-time turf winner including off 5lb higher over C&D (good to firm) last spring; well held at Wolverhampton when last seen in March but that was after a good spell on AW including a Kempton win; has good record when fresh and he needs a close look back from four months off.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Made a successful 2yo debut but he was soon in deep water and his form deteriorated; seen only twice in 2025 but he's been placed a couple of times this season including when third over C&D (good to firm) on his penultimate run; looks interesting on that form and he's not ruled out off a dangerous mark.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Exposed mare who is 6-45 on turf; beaten in claimers on final two runs in Ireland in May and was well held in an Epsom handicap (6f, good; 33-1) on recent stable debut; has something to prove and all of her wins have been over further.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Eight of his 14 wins have come at Windsor and latest was a comfortable success over C&D (good to firm) three weeks ago; up 5lb but he still looks on a workable mark and has been in good form since April; big player.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two turf wins last summer and three AW wins this year, the latest at Wolverhampton in April; ran well when third of five at Salisbury next time but three lesser runs have followed and he needs to raise his game.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: His last win was in December 2024 but he's finished runner-up over C&D (good to firm) in his last two starts including behind Lequinto on his penultimate run; didn't get much luck when returned to hold-up tactics last Monday and he looks interesting with Oisin Murphy booked again.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won at Dundalk (6f, AW) in January but he's not come close to that form since and was was well held on recent stable debut; now goes back up in trip but he's 1-19 on turf and others are preferred.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won on Bath debut (5f, good to soft) last October before a second at Wolverhampton 11 days later; well held on handicap debut over C&D last month but he may have needed that run and he's still unexposed; not ruled out.

RaceName: Skybet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Narrow success in April took his C&D record to 2-3; has remained in form elsewhere since and his latest effort, in Class 2 at Epsom, can be marked up (hampered then rallied for third place); respected back here.

RaceName: Skybet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Back in form with three placed efforts this summer; the latest one took his C&D record to 1113; this front-running 8yo remains just 1lb above last winning mark; solid chance.

RaceName: Skybet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: In-form 6yo whose two attempts over C&D comprise a success in May and 1l second latest start; holds a fighting chance off unaltered mark; defied a stone higher in 2024.

RaceName: Skybet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Scored over C&D in June; below par returned to this scenario last time but that race possibly came too soon after a good effort three days earlier; may rebound.

RaceName: Skybet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Ties in with some of today's rivals on recent C&D form but is still seeking a first success at this trip; has gained all Beverley wins over the extended 1m.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Back to form with 1.5l third of 15 behind in-form Arch Enemy at Killarney (11.5f, good) five days ago; might prefer a bit of an ease in the ground, but place claims again off the same mark.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Heavy defeat when last seen on the Flat at Dundalk in January; pulled up over hurdles on return from 168 days latest; remote third over C&D last June off 8lb higher, so can't fully discount but needs to rediscover best.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Admirable veteran gained a fourth turf win on the Flat at Sligo in May (1m6f, yld-sft); has come up short off revised mark since but has C&D form figures of 124 and the win came off 6lb higher than he is here; could go well.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins in Britain for Ian Williams at 1m2f on all sorts of ground; yet to find form for this stable; new headgear combination tried after 8.5l defeat at Fairyhouse latest.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Pulled up in a bumper here; minor promise in AW maidens; soundly beaten in first two handicaps at Dundalk and can only be watched after 129-day break.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Solid strike rate of 4-18 on the AW but 0-11 on turf and has yet to trouble the judge; beaten 22l at Bellewstown 17 days ago; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time AW winner is 0-19 on turf; hasn't looked like ending that winless run on last three starts, beaten 13l at Navan on latest; others hold stronger claims.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 1m5f (good) Leopardstown handicap off basement mark of 40 on final start for Mags Mullins in May; solid first two runs for this yard; 5l third of 16 at Bellewstown (1m4f, good) and denied by short head at Down Royal (1m5f, good) three days ago; big chance off 2lb lower.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Dual hurdles winner is 0-10 on the Flat (0-4 on turf); beaten 45l on stable debut after long layoff at Tramore latest; may need another run or two to find form; cheekpieces on.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Has placed in three of his five runs this season, including when a 0.75l second to Arch Enemy (who has won again since) on latest at Navan (1m2f, good); also has placed form at 1m4f so decent chance he'll stay this far; place shout at least.

RaceName: Racing, Sports & Games At Tote.ie Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden is 0-5 on turf but her last run at Killarney was encouraging (stayed on well over 1m for a 5l fifth); small yard has been in sensational form and it would be no surprise to see this filly run a big race upped markedly in trip; 1lb wrong but rider's claim negates that.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: Generally progressive filly who has form figures of 42321 and she landed the odds with a runaway win in a Pontefract novice (1m, good) three weeks ago; this is tougher off a revised mark back in a handicap but she should have more to offer at this new trip; respected.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to connections' Oaks winner Soul Sister; close second to useful rival at Kempton (1m, AW) on sole 2yo start; disappointing on Sandown reappearance in April but she bounced back with an emphatic win at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) last month; looks on fair mark now switched to a handicap and she's open to more progress back on turf; interesting contender.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: Reappeared with a clearcut win at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in April but she's lost her way with heavy defeats at Sandown and Newbury in last two runs; has plenty to prove again and overall record is now 1-14.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: 250,000euros yearling; made it third-time lucky with an easy win at Southwell (1m, AW) in May and she had a tough task on handicap debut at Royal Ascot (Sandringham) last month; finished in rear in that race but she still has potential and is bred to be suited by this new trip; could resume her progress back in calmer waters.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when scoring on Wolverhampton reappearance (8.6f, AW) in May; backed that up with a close call at this track (1m, good to firm) but her progress stalled with a disappointing run at Newmarket last time; has bit to prove again after that and she needs to bounce back with tongue-tie added; stablemate of Molo Del Palazzo.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: Four-race maiden but she started her handicap career with a creditable second at Lingfield (1m2f, good; 20-1) last month; nudged up 1lb but she kept on well last time and is open to more progress at this trip; in the mix.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Support The Big Bear Voyage Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)
Spotlight: Started this season with two wins at Nottingham (8.3f, good) but she's been well held in her last two starts; didn't get the best of runs at Sandown last time but she needs to find more on this step up to 1m2f.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time C&D scorer, including in this race two years ago; the race didn't pan out favourably when returned to this scenario last time (form dipped); 1lb below last winning mark and not ruled out.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 0-10 since debut win but he proved resurgent in first-time tongue-tie and cheekpieces at Windsor (third start for new yard) most recently; may build on that effort; opening mark was 30lb higher; respected.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Retains vitality aged nine, having won at Doncaster in May; below par returned to that venue last time, however, and others are preferred.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Four-race maiden who isn't particularly solid on form and hasn't made the frame yet; however, this switch to handicap level may enable her to make a bigger impression.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Won off higher marks in the first third of 2025 but holds poor claims on his 2026 form for new stable; chance of revival depends on the effects of first-time headgear.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Far from solid on his sprinting form; chance depends on the effects of new trip and switch to visor (wore cheekpieces last time).

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: 12-race maiden; inconsistent this season for new yard but ran well (close second) in 3yo handicap over C&D most recently; possibilities, provided he backs up that effort.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden who is out of a Beverley winner, in cheekpieces, for his connections; not fully convincing on form but is interesting in first-time cheekpieces kept to this course, with improvement possible.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo filly whose form has rather levelled off; however, may show the benefit of new headgear, having gained her sole success when wearing a first-time visor (retained largely since).

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Harry Kelly; creditable efforts on both Flat starts for this yard; 0.5l third of 8 at Bellewstown in April 2025; 7.25l fourth after layoff at Roscommon latest (1m4f, good); sharper for that and place chance.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Point-to-point and hunter chase winner; modest Flat debut at Leopardstown over 1m7f; a bit better when a 10.5l fourth over 1m6f at Killarney latest; should get a handicap mark after this.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner at Kilbeggan in May; form otherwise poor, including heavy defeats in both bumpers; tailed off on handicap hurdle debut at Tramore on latest; best watched on Flat debut.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt in bumpers at Wexford on latest (2m1f, good); nicely bred (dam a half-sister to Casamento) and could challenge for place money on Flat debut.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Chase and hurdle winner ran well on latest when runner-up in the Connacht National at Roscommon; beaten 12l when fourth on sole Flat start over 1m2f at Leopardstown in July 2024; would prefer softer ground.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: A winner over fences and hurdles; not disgraced on belated Flat debut in a Tramore maiden on latest (1m4f, good), but much more needed to take this.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Built on mildly promising debut at Gowran (9.5f, soft) to chase home Ashikita when upped to 1m4f at Roscommon (good); an extra furlong here should pose no problems and she looks the main danger to Nuit; cheekpieces go on.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: 0-11 but has run some excellent races in defeat in stakes company; beaten 7.75l in 1m6f (good) Fairyhouse Group 3 on latest; rated 96 and a surprise if she didn't win this.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Maiden
Spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles; okay efforts over fences the last twice and now makes her belated Flat debut; probably best watched.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time course winner (1m-1m2f) but his last success was over 2m on AW last March; on dangerous mark but he's returned from a lengthy absence with two low-key runs this summer including over C&D; bit to prove and needs to rediscover his spark.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark with tongue-tie added at Kempton (1m, AW) in April 2025, but he had a long spell on the sidelines before finishing tailed off in two runs this summer; still has low-mileage but he needs a revival.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form with a brave win at Epsom (1m2f, good) 19 days ago and he backed that up with a creditable third at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm); a 2lb rise has now kicked in but he remains feasibly treated and should go well again.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore
Spotlight: Won 1m handicaps at Bath (good to firm) and Lingfield (AW) last year; well treated on that form but she's been generally disappointing since her last success and was beaten 4l when third at Bath last time; needs to raise her game and she's untried at this trip.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: His last win was two years ago but he's been placed in all three runs this season including close calls over C&D (good to firm) and at Ffos Las (1m2f, good); remains well handicapped on his old form and he should make another bold bid.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row at Nottingham (1m2f, good/good to soft) last summer but she's been out of sorts this season and failed to beat a rival at Nottingham last time; now 8lb lower than for her last success but she needs to turn things around.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time AW winner who is 0-12 on grass but he's been placed on turf in his last three runs including when runner-up at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm) 16 days ago; on same mark here and he should be in the thick of things again.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive once handicapping over this trip last year, winning four from six (three on Polytrack), latterly off a 2lb lower mark; held in three runs this season but she wasn't beaten far at Lingfield last time and that was more like it; not ruled out.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up off this mark at Newbury (1m, good) in May but slow starts can be an issue and he's been well held in his last two runs; didn't get much luck at Nottingham last time but he's not easy to predict and others are more persuasive.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: No win in his first 18 starts but he made the breakthrough in a handicap here (11.4f, good to firm) last month and backed that up with a creditable third on his return visit seven days later; now drops back in trip but he ran well over C&D in May and he still looks on a workable mark; shortlisted.

RaceName: ECA 125th Year Anniversary Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent ten-race maiden who was tailed off in a Bath handicap (11.6f, good) last time; others preferred.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
OwnerName: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr
Spotlight: Veteran whose C&D form features a success in 2025 and a couple of second-place finishes this spring; fighting chance at this level, provided he bounces back (out of the money in last two runs).

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 4yo maiden who is far from solid on his form for current yard; posted one of his better efforts last time but isn't certain to back it up.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Has plenty of Beverley form, most recently a keeping-on third over 7.5f on penultimate start; however, this three-time Newcastle AW winner is only 1-27 on turf, the win registered in 2023.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Campaigned mostly over middle distances for current yard, winning twice (including at Beverley) last summer; recent form includes a creditable effort at this course; major player in this field, provided he copes with drop back in trip.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 4yo maiden who has become far from consistent; creditable second over C&D in June but looks no certainty to reproduce that form.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Five AW wins; form has dipped sharply since returned to turf, taking record in this sphere to 0-24; big revival is required.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Four-race maiden who is the only unexposed contender in this field; failed to get home in 1m2f handicap at Beverley most recently, finishing fourth of six; interesting back down in trip, with improvement possible.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Added to his two turf wins when finishing well to score on AW at Lingfield (7f) last October; hasn't hit the same level in two subsequent runs but he returned after wind surgery with a fair third in a Class 4 at Salisbury (7f, good) last month; looks worth another try at this trip and he's in the mix on this drop back in grade.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Pulled up on hurdle debut when last seen but he's a dual AW winner who is well handicapped on his second at Doncaster (1m, good) last summer; has run well when fresh in the past and he needs watching in market on reappearance; refused to enter stalls in last intended start.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs R Coleman And G Glendining
Spotlight: Turf and dual AW winner who ran near her best when runner-up from wide draw at Kempton (1m, AW; 11-4 favourite) 12 days ago; that was behind an improver who made all and she's a key player off unchanged mark.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Turned things around with a breakthrough win at Wolverhampton (8.5f) in May; disappointing at Ffos Las next time but he bounced back with an eye-catching fifth at Kempton 12 days ago; remains well handicapped on old form and has claims if this sets up for his closing style.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February and runner-up at Southwell (1m, AW) and Bath (1m, firm) in the spring; still looks on a workable mark but his form nosedived at Brighton last time and he needs to bounce back; one of three runners for Tony Carroll.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden who has not finished closer than fifth and was never dangerous at Bath (1m, good) last month; wasn't disgraced behind a major improver last time but he needs to produce a more dynamic effort here.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: 11-time winner, with five on turf and six on AW; his last success was off 2lb higher at Southwell in March but he's struggled since and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Racing Again Next Tuesday Handicap
Spotlight: Scored by 7l under a very promising amateur rider last time, completing a Pontefract double (1m2f, good) and taking record this term to 2-2; has won over C&D; surging back up the weights but arrives in great form.

RaceName: Racing Again Next Tuesday Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Steadily improving 4yo; prevailed narrowly at Doncaster (good to firm) most recently, making it five wins from his last seven runs (7f/1m) and taking record under Ethan Tindall to 4-5; new trip is the question mark.

RaceName: Racing Again Next Tuesday Handicap
Spotlight: Placed in four of her six starts; ran well in first-time cheekpieces at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm) most recently, doing best work at the finish; possibilities upped further in distance with the headgear retained.

RaceName: Racing Again Next Tuesday Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed by half a length in C&D event (good to firm) last week, ending a long losing sequence and taking Beverley record to 14321; incurs 4lb penalty but remains well treated on lots of form; solid chance.

RaceName: Racing Again Next Tuesday Handicap
Spotlight: Reasonably consistent five-race maiden for Ed Walker; seemingly outstayed over 1m2f on soft ground last time; sold for 9,500gns since; could go well, assuming she remains in form for new stable.
